# Frontend Engineer Agent

## Overview
This document outlines the responsibilities and skills required for a Frontend Engineer specializing in Next.js dashboard implementations.

## Responsibilities
- Develop and implement responsive dashboards using Next.js.
- Ensure that code adheres to TypeScript standards and leverages the best practices for type safety.
- Design components that are accessible to all users, following WAI-ARIA and WCAG guidelines.
- Collaborate with UX/UI designers to create visually appealing and user-friendly interfaces.
- Perform code reviews and maintain coding standards across the team.

## Skills Required
- **Proficiency in Next.js**: Experience with server-side rendering and static site generation.
- **TypeScript**: Strong understanding of TypeScript with a focus on type definitions and interfaces for improved code maintainability.
- **Accessibility**: Knowledge of web accessibility standards and practices to create inclusive user experiences.
- **Component Design**: Ability to build modular and reusable components using modern React patterns.
- **Responsive UI**: Familiarity with CSS-in-JS libraries and responsive design techniques to ensure usability across devices.

## Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
- Code quality assessed through code reviews.
- Accessibility standard compliance in delivered products.
- User satisfaction evaluated through feedback on dashboard usability.

## Conclusion
The Frontend Engineer plays a vital role in creating intuitive and accessible dashboard experiences for users. The ideal candidate will be proactive in integrating TypeScript, accessibility standards, and responsive design principles into their work.
